---

### 1. "Why don't scientists trust atoms? Because they make up everything!"
**Γ¡É 6/10** ΓÇö A solid grandpa joke. It's the *atomic* unit of "dad humor" ΓÇö fundamental, inescapable, and it's been around since the Big Bang. We've all heard it, but it still lands. Barely.

---

### 2. "Why did the scarecrow win an award? Because he was outstanding in his field!"
**Γ¡É 7/10** ΓÇö Now THAT'S how you work a pun! Double meaning, clean delivery. He was truly outstanding... unlike this comedian's fashion choices.

---

### 3. "I told my wife she was drawing her eyebrows too high. She looked surprised."
**Γ¡É 9/10** ΓÇö *chef's kiss* The visual gag writes itself. Short, punchy, and the punchline IS the setup. This one genuinely raised MY eyebrows. Well played. ≡ƒæÅ

---

### 4. "Why do programmers prefer dark mode? Because light attracts bugs!"
**Γ¡É 8/10** ΓÇö Finally, a joke for the nerds in the back row! The double meaning of "bugs" is genuinely clever. Would've been a 10, but half the audience is already Googling what dark mode is.

---

### 5. "I asked my dog what two minus two is. He said nothing."
**Γ¡É 8/10** ΓÇö Deceptively brilliant. The "said nothing" works on TWO levels ΓÇö zero, and a dog's silence. Underrated gem. Your dog is the real comedian here. ≡ƒÉò

---

### 6. "Why did the bicycle fall over? Because it was two-tired!"
**Γ¡É 4/10** ΓÇö Oh no. NO. We rode that joke hard and put it away wet. *throws popcorn* This one's been recycled more times than a bike at a rental shop. You're better than this!

---

### 7. "Why can't you give Elsa a balloon? Because she'll just let it go!"
**Γ¡É 7/10** ΓÇö Okay, cute. A Disney crossover pun that actually works. It's been floating around since 2013, but it still has a little lift. Points for the cultural reference!

---

### 8. "I'm reading a book about anti-gravity. It's impossible to put down."
**Γ¡É 9/10** ΓÇö Now THIS is comedy craftsmanship! Wordplay so clean it defies physics. Conceptually satisfying AND funny. The best closer of the night ΓÇö it elevates the whole set!

---

|---|------|-------|
| 1 | Atoms | Γ¡É 6/10 |
| 2 | Scarecrow | Γ¡É 7/10 |
| 3 | Eyebrows | Γ¡É 9/10 |
| 4 | Dark Mode | Γ¡É 8/10 |
| 5 | Dog Math | Γ¡É 8/10 |
| 6 | Bicycle | Γ¡É 4/10 |
| 7 | Elsa | Γ¡É 7/10 |
| 8 | Anti-Gravity | Γ¡É 9/10 |

**≡ƒÄ» Overall Set Score: 7.25/10** ΓÇö A genuinely entertaining set! Two absolute bangers (#3 and #8), a few solid mid-card jokes, and one absolutely *tire*-d clunker holding you back. Drop the bicycle joke and you're headlining Vegas, kid. ≡ƒÄ░

toolArgs.prompt:
The stand-up comedian told these jokes across their set:

1. Why don't scientists trust atoms? Because they make up everything!
2. Why did the scarecrow win an award? Because he was outstanding in his field!
3. I told my wife she was drawing her eyebrows too high. She looked surprised.
4. Why do programmers prefer dark mode? Because light attracts bugs!
5. I asked my dog what two minus two is. He said nothing.
6. Why did the bicycle fall over? Because it was two-tired!
7. Why can't you give Elsa a balloon? Because she'll just let it go!
8. I'm reading a book about anti-gravity. It's impossible to put down.

Please rate these jokes!
